# forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries }

`forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries`



## Classes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries) | Recursive autoregressive forecaster for multiple time series. |

### ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries(
    estimator=None,
    lags=None,
    window_features=None,
    encoding='ordinal',
    transformer_series=None,
    transformer_exog=None,
    weight_func=None,
    series_weights=None,
    differentiation=None,
    dropna_from_series=False,
    fit_kwargs=None,
    binner_kwargs=None,
    forecaster_id=None,
    regressor=None,
)
```

Recursive autoregressive forecaster for multiple time series.

This class turns any estimator compatible with the scikit-learn API into a
recursive autoregressive (multi-step) forecaster for multiple series.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name               | Type                                                                                             | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Default     |
|--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------|
| estimator          | [object](`object`)                                                                               | An instance of an estimator or pipeline compatible with the scikit-learn API.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | `None`      |
| lags               | ([int](`int`), [list](`list`), [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`), [range](`range`), None) | Lags used as predictors. Index starts at 1, so lag 1 is equal to t-1. - `int`: include lags from 1 to `lags` (included). - `list`, `1d numpy ndarray` or `range`: include only lags present in   `lags`, all elements must be int. - `None`: no lags are included as predictors.                                        | `None`      |
| window_features    | ([object](`object`), [list](`list`), None)                                                       | Instance or list of instances used to create window features from the original time series.                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | `None`      |
| encoding           | ([str](`str`), None)                                                                             | Encoding used to identify the different series. - `'ordinal'`: single column with integer values [0, n_series - 1]. - `'ordinal_category'`: same as 'ordinal' but with pandas.category dtype. - `'onehot'`: binary column for each series. - `None`: no series identification column is created. Defaults to 'ordinal'. | `'ordinal'` |
| transformer_series | ([transformer](`transformer`), [dict](`dict`), None)                                             | Preprocessor compatible with scikit-learn (fit/transform/inverse_transform). Applied to each series before training. ColumnTransformers are not allowed. - Single transformer: cloned and applied to all series. - Dict: different transformer per series.                                                              | `None`      |
| transformer_exog   | ([transformer](`transformer`), None)                                                             | Preprocessor for exogenous variables. `inverse_transform` is not available for ColumnTransformers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `None`      |
| weight_func        | ([Callable](`typing.Callable`), [dict](`dict`), None)                                            | Function defining weights for each sample based on index. Only used if estimator supports `sample_weight`. - Single function: applied to all series. - Dict: `{'series_name': Callable}`, others get weight 1.                                                                                                          | `None`      |
| series_weights     | ([dict](`dict`), None)                                                                           | Weights per series `{'series_name': float}`. Applied if estimator supports `sample_weight`. Others get weight 1. If `None`, all levels have weight 1.                                                                                                                                                                   | `None`      |
| differentiation    | ([int](`int`), [dict](`dict`), None)                                                             | Order of differencing applied to series. - `int`: same order for all series. - `dict`: different order per series (keys are series names). - `None`: no differencing.                                                                                                                                                   | `None`      |
| dropna_from_series | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                   | If `True`, drops rows with NaNs in X_train/y_train. If `False`, leaves NaNs and warns. Defaults to `False`.                                                                                                                                                                                                             | `False`     |
| fit_kwargs         | ([dict](`dict`), None)                                                                           | Additional arguments for the estimator's `fit` method.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | `None`      |
| binner_kwargs      | ([dict](`dict`), None)                                                                           | Arguments for `QuantileBinner` used for residuals. Includes `n_bins`, `method`, `subsample`, `random_state`, `dtype`.                                                                                                                                                                                                   | `None`      |
| forecaster_id      | ([str](`str`), [int](`int`), None)                                                               | Identifier for the forecaster.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | `None`      |
| regressor          | [object](`object`)                                                                               | **Deprecated**, alias for `estimator`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | `None`      |

#### Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

| Name                               | Type                                                                                                      | Description                                                                                                                     |
|------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| estimator                          | [object](`object`)                                                                                        | Estimator or pipeline compatible with scikit-learn.                                                                             |
| lags                               | [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)                                                                  | Lags used as predictors.                                                                                                        |
| lags_names                         | [list](`list`)                                                                                            | Names of the lags used as predictors.                                                                                           |
| max_lag                            | [int](`int`)                                                                                              | Maximum lag included in `lags`.                                                                                                 |
| window_features                    | [list](`list`)                                                                                            | Classes used to create window features.                                                                                         |
| window_features_names              | [list](`list`)                                                                                            | Names of the window features in `X_train`.                                                                                      |
| window_features_class_names        | [list](`list`)                                                                                            | Names of classes for window features.                                                                                           |
| max_size_window_features           | [int](`int`)                                                                                              | Maximum window size for window features.                                                                                        |
| window_size                        | [int](`int`)                                                                                              | Required window size for predictors. Max of `max_lag` and `max_size_window_features`, plus differentiation order if applicable. |
| encoding                           | [str](`str`)                                                                                              | Encoding used to identify the different series.                                                                                 |
| encoder                            | [sklearn](`sklearn`).[preprocessing](`sklearn.preprocessing`)                                             | Scikit-learn encoder for series.                                                                                                |
| encoding_mapping_                  | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Mapping of the encoding for series identities.                                                                                  |
| transformer_series                 | ([transformer](`transformer`), [dict](`dict`))                                                            | Transformer(s) for the series.                                                                                                  |
| transformer_series_                | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Internal dictionary of series transformers.                                                                                     |
| transformer_exog                   | [transformer](`transformer`)                                                                              | Transformer for exogenous variables.                                                                                            |
| weight_func                        | ([Callable](`typing.Callable`), [dict](`dict`))                                                           | Weighting function(s).                                                                                                          |
| weight_func_                       | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Internal dictionary of weighting functions.                                                                                     |
| source_code_weight_func            | ([str](`str`), [dict](`dict`))                                                                            | Source code of weighting functions.                                                                                             |
| series_weights                     | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Weights associated with each series.                                                                                            |
| series_weights_                    | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Internal dictionary of series weights.                                                                                          |
| differentiation                    | ([int](`int`), [dict](`dict`))                                                                            | Differencing order applied to series.                                                                                           |
| differentiation_max                | [int](`int`)                                                                                              | Maximum order of differentiation.                                                                                               |
| differentiator                     | ([TimeSeriesDifferentiator](`spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.TimeSeriesDifferentiator`), [dict](`dict`)) | Differentiation objects.                                                                                                        |
| differentiator_                    | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Internal dictionary of differentiators.                                                                                         |
| dropna_from_series                 | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                            | Whether to drop NaNs from training matrices.                                                                                    |
| last_window_                       | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Last training window per series in original scale.                                                                              |
| index_type_                        | [type](`type`)                                                                                            | Type of training index.                                                                                                         |
| index_freq_                        | [str](`str`)                                                                                              | Frequency of training index.                                                                                                    |
| training_range_                    | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | First/last training index values per series.                                                                                    |
| series_names_in_                   | [list](`list`)                                                                                            | Series names provided during training.                                                                                          |
| exog_in_                           | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                            | `True` if trained with exogenous variables.                                                                                     |
| exog_names_in_                     | [list](`list`)                                                                                            | Names of exogenous variables used.                                                                                              |
| exog_type_in_                      | [type](`type`)                                                                                            | Type of exogenous data used.                                                                                                    |
| exog_dtypes_in_                    | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Exogenous data types before transformation.                                                                                     |
| exog_dtypes_out_                   | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Exogenous data types after transformation.                                                                                      |
| X_train_series_names_in_           | [list](`list`)                                                                                            | Series names in the internal `X_train`.                                                                                         |
| X_train_window_features_names_out_ | [list](`list`)                                                                                            | Window feature names in `X_train`.                                                                                              |
| X_train_exog_names_out_            | [list](`list`)                                                                                            | Exogenous variable names in `X_train`.                                                                                          |
| X_train_features_names_out_        | [list](`list`)                                                                                            | All feature column names in `X_train`.                                                                                          |
| fit_kwargs                         | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Arguments passed to estimator's `fit`.                                                                                          |
| in_sample_residuals_               | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Training residuals (up to 10k per series).                                                                                      |
| in_sample_residuals_by_bin_        | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Binned in-sample residuals.                                                                                                     |
| out_sample_residuals_              | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Non-training residuals (up to 10k per series).                                                                                  |
| out_sample_residuals_by_bin_       | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Binned out-of-sample residuals.                                                                                                 |
| binner                             | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | `QuantileBinner` objects per series.                                                                                            |
| binner_intervals_                  | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Binning intervals per series.                                                                                                   |
| binner_kwargs                      | [dict](`dict`)                                                                                            | Arguments used for `QuantileBinner`.                                                                                            |
| creation_date                      | [str](`str`)                                                                                              | Forecaster creation date.                                                                                                       |
| is_fitted                          | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                            | `True` if the forecaster has been fitted.                                                                                       |
| fit_date                           | [str](`str`)                                                                                              | Date of last fit.                                                                                                               |
| spotforecast_version               | [str](`str`)                                                                                              | Version of the library used.                                                                                                    |
| python_version                     | [str](`str`)                                                                                              | Python version used.                                                                                                            |
| forecaster_id                      | ([str](`str`), [int](`int`))                                                                              | Forecaster identifier.                                                                                                          |

#### Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

Weights are used to control the influence of each observation.
`ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries` accepts two types of weights:
- `series_weights`: Controls relative importance of each series.
- `weight_func`: Controls relative importance based on index values.
If both are provided, they are multiplied. Negative weights are not allowed.

#### Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [create_predict_X](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.create_predict_X) | Create the predictors needed to predict `steps` ahead. |
| [create_sample_weights](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.create_sample_weights) | Create weights for each observation according to the forecaster's attributes |
| [create_train_X_y](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.create_train_X_y) | Create training matrices from multiple time series and exogenous variables. |
| [fit](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.fit) | Training Forecaster. |
| [get_feature_importances](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.get_feature_importances) | Return feature importances of the estimator. |
| [predict](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict) | Predict n steps ahead. |
| [predict_bootstrapping](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_bootstrapping) | Generate multiple forecasting predictions using a bootstrapping process. |
| [predict_dist](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_dist) | Fit a given probability distribution for each step. |
| [predict_interval](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_interval) | Predict n steps ahead and estimate prediction intervals. |
| [predict_quantiles](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_quantiles) | Calculate the specified quantiles for each step. |
| [set_fit_kwargs](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_fit_kwargs) | Set new values for the additional keyword arguments passed to the `fit` method. |
| [set_in_sample_residuals](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_in_sample_residuals) | Set in-sample residuals in case they were not calculated during training. |
| [set_lags](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_lags) | Set new value to the attribute `lags`. |
| [set_out_sample_residuals](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_out_sample_residuals) | Set new values to the attribute `out_sample_residuals_`. |
| [set_params](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_params) | Set new values to the parameters of the scikit-learn model. |
| [set_window_features](#spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_window_features) | Set new value to the attribute `window_features`. |

##### create_predict_X { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.create_predict_X }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.create_predict_X(
    steps,
    levels=None,
    last_window=None,
    exog=None,
    suppress_warnings=False,
    check_inputs=True,
)
```

Create the predictors needed to predict `steps` ahead.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name              | Type                                                                                                             | Description                                                       | Default    |
|-------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| steps             | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Number of steps to predict.                                       | _required_ |
| levels            | ([str](`str`), [list](`list`), None)                                                                             | Time series to be predicted. Defaults to `None`.                  | `None`     |
| last_window       | ([pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), None)                                                           | Series values for predictors. Defaults to `None`.                 | `None`     |
| exog              | ([pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`), None) | Exogenous variable/s included as predictor/s. Defaults to `None`. | `None`     |
| suppress_warnings | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, suppress warnings. Defaults to `False`.                | `False`    |
| check_inputs      | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, check input for warnings/errors. Defaults to `True`.   | `True`     |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                           | Description                                              |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | pd.DataFrame: Long-format DataFrame with the predictors. |

##### create_sample_weights { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.create_sample_weights }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.create_sample_weights(
    series_names_in_,
    X_train,
)
```

Create weights for each observation according to the forecaster's attributes
`series_weights` and `weight_func`. The resulting weights are product of both
types of weights.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name             | Type                                           | Description                                           | Default    |
|------------------|------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| series_names_in_ | [list](`list`)                                 | Names of the series (levels) used during training.    | _required_ |
| X_train          | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | Dataframe created with the `create_train_X_y` method. | _required_ |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                     | Description                                 |
|--------|------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|
|        | [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) | np.ndarray: Weights to use in `fit` method. |

##### create_train_X_y { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.create_train_X_y }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.create_train_X_y(
    series,
    exog=None,
    suppress_warnings=False,
)
```

Create training matrices from multiple time series and exogenous variables.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name              | Type                                                                                                             | Description                                                                             | Default    |
|-------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| series            | ([pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`))                                                 | Training time series.                                                                   | _required_ |
| exog              | ([pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`), None) | Exogenous variable/s included as predictor/s. Defaults to `None`.                       | `None`     |
| suppress_warnings | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, skforecast warnings will be suppressed during creation. Defaults to `False`. | `False`    |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                                                                         | Description                                                                                                                                                               |
|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| tuple  | [tuple](`tuple`)\[[pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`)\] | A tuple containing: - X_train (pd.DataFrame): Training values (predictors). - y_train (pd.Series): Values (target) of the time series related   to each row of `X_train`. |

###### Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

See `_create_train_X_y` for detailed information on input types.

##### fit { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.fit }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.fit(
    series,
    exog=None,
    store_last_window=True,
    store_in_sample_residuals=False,
    random_state=123,
    suppress_warnings=False,
)
```

Training Forecaster.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name                      | Type                                                                                                             | Description                                                                                                          | Default    |
|---------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| series                    | ([pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`))                                                 | Training time series.                                                                                                | _required_ |
| exog                      | ([pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`), None) | Exogenous variable/s included as predictor/s. Defaults to `None`.                                                    | `None`     |
| store_last_window         | ([bool](`bool`), [list](`list`))                                                                                 | Whether or not to store the last window (`last_window_`) of training data. Defaults to `True`.                       | `True`     |
| store_in_sample_residuals | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, in-sample residuals will be stored in the forecaster object after fitting. Defaults to `False`.           | `False`    |
| random_state              | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Set a seed for the random generator so that the stored sample residuals are always deterministic. Defaults to `123`. | `123`      |
| suppress_warnings         | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, skforecast warnings will be suppressed during training. Defaults to `False`.                              | `False`    |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description   |
|--------|--------|---------------|
|        | None   | None          |

###### Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

- If `series` is a wide-format pandas DataFrame, each column represents
  a different time series.
- If `series` is a long-format pandas DataFrame with a MultiIndex, the
  first level of the index must contain the series IDs.
- If series is a dictionary, each key must be a series ID.
- If `exog` is a wide-format pandas DataFrame, it must share the same
  index type as series.
- If `exog` is a long-format pandas Series or DataFrame with a MultiIndex,
  the first level contains the series IDs.
- If `exog` is a dictionary, each key must correspond to a series ID.

##### get_feature_importances { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.get_feature_importances }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.get_feature_importances(
    sort_importance=True,
)
```

Return feature importances of the estimator.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name            | Type           | Description                                                                       | Default   |
|-----------------|----------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| sort_importance | [bool](`bool`) | If `True`, sorts the feature importances in descending order. Defaults to `True`. | `True`    |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                           | Description                                                       |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | pd.DataFrame: Feature importances associated with each predictor. |

##### predict { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict(
    steps,
    levels=None,
    last_window=None,
    exog=None,
    suppress_warnings=False,
    check_inputs=True,
)
```

Predict n steps ahead.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name              | Type                                                                                                             | Description                                                       | Default    |
|-------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| steps             | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Number of steps to predict.                                       | _required_ |
| levels            | ([str](`str`), [list](`list`), None)                                                                             | Time series to be predicted. Defaults to `None`.                  | `None`     |
| last_window       | ([pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), None)                                                           | Series values for predictors. Defaults to `None`.                 | `None`     |
| exog              | ([pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`), None) | Exogenous variable/s included as predictor/s. Defaults to `None`. | `None`     |
| suppress_warnings | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, suppress warnings. Defaults to `False`.                | `False`    |
| check_inputs      | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, check input for warnings/errors. Defaults to `True`.   | `True`     |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                           | Description                                               |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | pd.DataFrame: Long-format DataFrame with the predictions. |

##### predict_bootstrapping { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_bootstrapping }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_bootstrapping(
    steps,
    levels=None,
    last_window=None,
    exog=None,
    n_boot=250,
    use_in_sample_residuals=True,
    use_binned_residuals=True,
    random_state=123,
    suppress_warnings=False,
)
```

Generate multiple forecasting predictions using a bootstrapping process.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name                    | Type                                                                                                             | Description                                                                      | Default    |
|-------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| steps                   | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Number of steps to predict.                                                      | _required_ |
| levels                  | ([str](`str`), [list](`list`), None)                                                                             | Time series to be predicted. Defaults to `None`.                                 | `None`     |
| last_window             | ([pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), None)                                                           | Series values used to create predictors. Defaults to `None`.                     | `None`     |
| exog                    | ([pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`), None) | Exogenous variable/s included as predictor/s. Defaults to `None`.                | `None`     |
| n_boot                  | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Number of bootstrapping iterations. Defaults to `250`.                           | `250`      |
| use_in_sample_residuals | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, use residuals from training data. Defaults to `True`.                 | `True`     |
| use_binned_residuals    | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, residuals are selected based on predicted values. Defaults to `True`. | `True`     |
| random_state            | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Seed for reproducibility. Defaults to `123`.                                     | `123`      |
| suppress_warnings       | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, suppress warnings. Defaults to `False`.                               | `False`    |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                           | Description                                                             |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | pd.DataFrame: Long-format DataFrame with the bootstrapping predictions. |

###### References {.doc-section .doc-section-references}

[1] Forecasting: Principles and Practice (3rd ed) Rob J Hyndman and George Athanasopoulos.
    https://otexts.com/fpp3/prediction-intervals.html

##### predict_dist { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_dist }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_dist(
    steps,
    distribution,
    levels=None,
    last_window=None,
    exog=None,
    n_boot=250,
    use_in_sample_residuals=True,
    use_binned_residuals=True,
    random_state=123,
    suppress_warnings=False,
)
```

Fit a given probability distribution for each step.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name                    | Type                                                                                                             | Description                                                                      | Default    |
|-------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| steps                   | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Number of steps to predict.                                                      | _required_ |
| distribution            | [object](`object`)                                                                                               | A distribution object from scipy.stats.                                          | _required_ |
| levels                  | ([str](`str`), [list](`list`), None)                                                                             | Time series to be predicted. Defaults to `None`.                                 | `None`     |
| last_window             | ([pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), None)                                                           | Series values used to create predictors. Defaults to `None`.                     | `None`     |
| exog                    | ([pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`), None) | Exogenous variable/s included as predictor/s. Defaults to `None`.                | `None`     |
| n_boot                  | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Number of bootstrapping iterations. Defaults to `250`.                           | `250`      |
| use_in_sample_residuals | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, use residuals from training data. Defaults to `True`.                 | `True`     |
| use_binned_residuals    | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, residuals are selected based on predicted values. Defaults to `True`. | `True`     |
| random_state            | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Seed for reproducibility. Defaults to `123`.                                     | `123`      |
| suppress_warnings       | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, suppress warnings. Defaults to `False`.                               | `False`    |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                           | Description                                                                     |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | pd.DataFrame: Long-format DataFrame with parameters of the fitted distribution. |

##### predict_interval { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_interval }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_interval(
    steps,
    levels=None,
    last_window=None,
    exog=None,
    method='conformal',
    interval=[5, 95],
    n_boot=250,
    use_in_sample_residuals=True,
    use_binned_residuals=True,
    random_state=123,
    suppress_warnings=False,
)
```

Predict n steps ahead and estimate prediction intervals.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name                    | Type                                                                                                             | Description                                                                                                        | Default       |
|-------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------|
| steps                   | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Number of steps to predict.                                                                                        | _required_    |
| levels                  | ([str](`str`), [list](`list`), None)                                                                             | Time series to be predicted. Defaults to `None`.                                                                   | `None`        |
| last_window             | ([pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), None)                                                           | Series values used to create predictors. Defaults to `None`.                                                       | `None`        |
| exog                    | ([pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`), None) | Exogenous variable/s included as predictor/s. Defaults to `None`.                                                  | `None`        |
| method                  | [str](`str`)                                                                                                     | Technique used to estimate prediction intervals. Options: 'bootstrapping', 'conformal'. Defaults to `'conformal'`. | `'conformal'` |
| interval                | ([float](`float`), [list](`list`), [tuple](`tuple`))                                                             | Confidence level of the prediction interval. Defaults to `[5, 95]`.                                                | `[5, 95]`     |
| n_boot                  | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Number of bootstrapping iterations. Defaults to `250`.                                                             | `250`         |
| use_in_sample_residuals | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, use residuals from training data. Defaults to `True`.                                                   | `True`        |
| use_binned_residuals    | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, residuals are selected based on predicted values. Defaults to `True`.                                   | `True`        |
| random_state            | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Seed for reproducibility. Defaults to `123`.                                                                       | `123`         |
| suppress_warnings       | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, suppress warnings. Defaults to `False`.                                                                 | `False`       |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                           | Description                                                         |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | pd.DataFrame: Long-format DataFrame with predictions and intervals. |

##### predict_quantiles { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_quantiles }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.predict_quantiles(
    steps,
    levels=None,
    last_window=None,
    exog=None,
    quantiles=[0.05, 0.5, 0.95],
    n_boot=250,
    use_in_sample_residuals=True,
    use_binned_residuals=True,
    random_state=123,
    suppress_warnings=False,
)
```

Calculate the specified quantiles for each step.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name                    | Type                                                                                                             | Description                                                                      | Default             |
|-------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|
| steps                   | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Number of steps to predict.                                                      | _required_          |
| levels                  | ([str](`str`), [list](`list`), None)                                                                             | Time series to be predicted. Defaults to `None`.                                 | `None`              |
| last_window             | ([pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), None)                                                           | Series values used to create predictors. Defaults to `None`.                     | `None`              |
| exog                    | ([pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`), None) | Exogenous variable/s included as predictor/s. Defaults to `None`.                | `None`              |
| quantiles               | ([list](`list`), [tuple](`tuple`))                                                                               | Sequence of quantiles to compute (0 to 1). Defaults to `[0.05, 0.5, 0.95]`.      | `[0.05, 0.5, 0.95]` |
| n_boot                  | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Number of bootstrapping iterations. Defaults to `250`.                           | `250`               |
| use_in_sample_residuals | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, use residuals from training data. Defaults to `True`.                 | `True`              |
| use_binned_residuals    | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, residuals are selected based on predicted values. Defaults to `True`. | `True`              |
| random_state            | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Seed for reproducibility. Defaults to `123`.                                     | `123`               |
| suppress_warnings       | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, suppress warnings. Defaults to `False`.                               | `False`             |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                           | Description                                                       |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | pd.DataFrame: Long-format DataFrame with the predicted quantiles. |

##### set_fit_kwargs { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_fit_kwargs }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_fit_kwargs(
    fit_kwargs,
)
```

Set new values for the additional keyword arguments passed to the `fit` method.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type           | Description                               | Default    |
|------------|----------------|-------------------------------------------|------------|
| fit_kwargs | [dict](`dict`) | Dict of the form {"argument": new_value}. | _required_ |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description   |
|--------|--------|---------------|
|        | None   | None          |

##### set_in_sample_residuals { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_in_sample_residuals }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_in_sample_residuals(
    series,
    exog=None,
    random_state=123,
    suppress_warnings=False,
)
```

Set in-sample residuals in case they were not calculated during training.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name              | Type                                                                                                             | Description                                                       | Default    |
|-------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| series            | ([pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`))                                                 | Training time series.                                             | _required_ |
| exog              | ([pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [dict](`dict`), None) | Exogenous variable/s included as predictor/s. Defaults to `None`. | `None`     |
| random_state      | [int](`int`)                                                                                                     | Seed for reproducibility. Defaults to `123`.                      | `123`      |
| suppress_warnings | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                   | If `True`, suppress warnings. Defaults to `False`.                | `False`    |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description   |
|--------|--------|---------------|
|        | None   | None          |

##### set_lags { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_lags }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_lags(
    lags=None,
)
```

Set new value to the attribute `lags`.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type                                                                                             | Description                                  | Default   |
|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------|-----------|
| lags   | ([int](`int`), [list](`list`), [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`), [range](`range`), None) | Lags used as predictors. Defaults to `None`. | `None`    |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description   |
|--------|--------|---------------|
|        | None   | None          |

##### set_out_sample_residuals { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_out_sample_residuals }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_out_sample_residuals(
    y_true,
    y_pred,
    append=False,
    random_state=123,
)
```

Set new values to the attribute `out_sample_residuals_`.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name         | Type           | Description                                                                   | Default    |
|--------------|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| y_true       | [dict](`dict`) | Dictionary with the true values of the time series.                           | _required_ |
| y_pred       | [dict](`dict`) | Dictionary with the predicted values of the time series.                      | _required_ |
| append       | [bool](`bool`) | If `True`, new residuals are added to the existing ones. Defaults to `False`. | `False`    |
| random_state | [int](`int`)   | Seed for reproducibility. Defaults to `123`.                                  | `123`      |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description   |
|--------|--------|---------------|
|        | None   | None          |

###### Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

Out-of-sample residuals can only be stored for series seen during fit.

##### set_params { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_params }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_params(
    params,
)
```

Set new values to the parameters of the scikit-learn model.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type           | Description       | Default    |
|--------|----------------|-------------------|------------|
| params | [dict](`dict`) | Parameter values. | _required_ |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description   |
|--------|--------|---------------|
|        | None   | None          |

##### set_window_features { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_window_features }

```python
forecaster.recursive._forecaster_recursive_multiseries.ForecasterRecursiveMultiSeries.set_window_features(
    window_features=None,
)
```

Set new value to the attribute `window_features`.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name            | Type                                       | Description                                                                       | Default   |
|-----------------|--------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| window_features | ([object](`object`), [list](`list`), None) | Instance or list of instances used to create window features. Defaults to `None`. | `None`    |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description   |
|--------|--------|---------------|
|        | None   | None          |
